authEvents.description: runs before sign-up/sign-in to customize the flow

header.headline: Build fullstack quickly and efficiently
header.tagline: Type-safe APIs, shared packages, auth & docs out of the box. Built for developer experience.

mobile.authIntro: better-auth client from @/libs/auth-client — same usage as on web.
mobile.intlIntro: Messages live in i18n/en.json and i18n/fr.json, loaded via IntlProvider.
mobile.loggingHint: Enable structured request logs when you turn logging on in the API guard.
mobile.fullDemoHint: Some steps are condensed on mobile; the web app includes longer walkthroughs.

Landing.selectFeaturePrompt: Select a feature above to see details.
Landing.templateSubtitle: Turbo Nest Trpc Next - Boiler plate monorepo (Bun)

descriptions.fullstackTypeSafety: Create a NestJS endpoint and call it from the frontend with complete type safety.
descriptions.shareResources: Create a simple React component and share it between your apps.
descriptions.storybook: Create your stories and develop your components in isolation.
descriptions.typedEnv: Validated and typed environment variables at startup (with dotenvx encrypted secrets).
descriptions.internationalisation: Same JSON messages as web via IntlProvider (Expo-friendly).
descriptions.apiProtection: AuthGuardRouter + @Public for public routes, protected routes require a session. Try both fetches below.
descriptions.autoDoc: We use trpc-ui to expose your tRPC API. The documentation is generated automatically from your routers and Zod schemas.
descriptions.authentication: Sign in, sign up and session management on the front-end with Better Auth.
descriptions.logging: Use AuthGuardRouter with logs: true to get formatted request logs (URL, meta, response) in the terminal.
descriptions.cleanEmails: In this project, email templates are built with react-email (tailwind + intl support) inside the `packages/emails` workspace. You only create React components for your emails; the package exports the rendered output used by the API.
descriptions.encryptedSecrets: Variables in .env.production are encrypted with dotenvx and can be safely committed. In CI, only set the private key from .env.keys.

step1.title: Fullstack type safety
step1.intro: Same AppRouter as web — trpc from @/libs/trpc-client.
step1.codeExample:
import { trpc } from '@/libs/trpc-client';

const { data } = trpc.app.hello.useQuery();
step1.codeHintBackend: Nest @Query + Zod output on the API; types flow to the client.
step1.clickToFetch: Tap the button to call trpc.app.hello

step8.title: Authentication with Better Auth
step8.googleConfigHint: Set EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_AUTH_CALLBACK_URL in .env to enable Google sign-in (same as web).
step8.accountNotSignedIn: Not signed in. Sign in above to view your account.
step8.resetCallbackSuccessDescription: You can now sign in with your new password.
step8.resetCallbackFormDescription: Submit a new password to complete reset.
step8.resetCallbackErrorDescription: Unable to reset your password. Please try again.

step7.title: Auto documentation
step7.intro: We use trpc-ui to expose your tRPC API. The documentation is generated automatically from your routers and Zod schemas.

step10.title: Encrypted .env with dotenvx
step10.intro: With dotenvx, your .env.production (and other env files) are automatically encrypted. You can safely push them to the repo—secrets are never stored in plain text.
step10.keysComment: Private decryption keys – backup with dotenvx ops backup
step10.ciHint: In your CI (GitHub Actions, Vercel, etc.), set only this env var. dotenvx will decrypt .env.production at runtime.
step10.ciOnlyKey: In CI: set DOTENV_PRIVATE_KEY_PRODUCTION (from .env.keys) as the only secret. No need to copy each variable.

step11.title: Clean emails
step11.explanation: In `packages/emails`, templates use `Tailwind` for styling and the `t(...)` helper to fetch intl/i18n messages (by locale + key + values). You just create React components; the API imports the rendered result.
step11.tailwindIntlHint: Support: Tailwind for email styling + intl/i18n inside the templates via the `t(...)` helper.

I18nExample.title: Hello world!
I18nExample.description: This text is translated from the same JSON files as the web app (Expo-friendly provider).
